summary: Generated PDF text overflows outside safe page margins.
prevention: Validate PDF output with long/adversarial text and safe margin checks before treating the artifact as complete.

summary: User-entered form data is cleared after a validation error.
prevention: Preserve valid user input and show field-specific recovery guidance.

summary: An element looks clickable but performs no meaningful action, or an important action lacks a clear affordance.
prevention: Apply affordance integrity rule: if it looks clickable, it must do something useful; if it does something useful, it should look clickable.

summary: Generated PDF text overflows outside safe page margins.
prevention: Validate PDF output with long/adversarial text and safe margin checks before treating the artifact as complete.

summary: A validation error clears valid user-entered form input.
prevention: Preserve valid user input and show field-specific recovery guidance.

summary: An element looks clickable but performs no meaningful action.
prevention: Apply affordance integrity rule: if it looks clickable, it must do something useful.

summary: A client-facing document or website includes a dead or misleading link.
prevention: Validate links before delivery and mark unavailable resources clearly.

summary: Multiple primary actions compete for the same workflow and confuse the user.
prevention: Use one canonical primary action unless duplicate placement has a clear workflow reason.

summary: Printed or PDF content overlaps, clips, or breaks awkwardly across page boundaries.
prevention: Validate print/PDF mode with realistic content before treating the artifact as complete.

summary: Public or client-facing material contains implementation-process, AI-process, SEO-process, assessment-process, or template-explanation copy instead of customer-facing language.
prevention: Review rendered pages/documents for process/meta copy and replace it with plain customer-facing facts before client review or publication.

summary: A generated website or document publishes fake samples, placeholder articles, invented testimonials, invented project examples, or unapproved proof as if it were real.
prevention: Remove placeholder proof from client/public delivery; require source evidence, permission, and attribution before publishing proof assets.

summary: Client-facing material makes or implies an unsupported service, service-area, credential, guarantee, direction, availability, delivery, or quote expectation claim.
prevention: Validate operational facts against client source material and mark missing details as pending instead of inventing or generalizing.

summary: A gallery, flyer, report, or proof section overuses similar examples or images, weakening trust and making the artifact feel generic.
prevention: Check visual/proof variety before delivery; request approved client assets when variety is missing instead of stretching near-duplicates.
